sglang.title: Add subprocess liveness monitor to detect scheduler crashes
sglang.author: Simon-Li
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Adds a subprocess liveness watchdog to detect scheduler crashes that bypass Python exception handlers (e.g., NCCL timeout causing C++ std::terminate()).
Problem: When a scheduler subprocess crashes at the C++ level, std::terminate() runs before Python exception handlers, leaving the main pro

sglang.title: fix: scheduler launch hang when non-current rank dies
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
During initialization the wait loop only monitored the rank it was currently reading from, so if another rank was killed the process would block on recv() indefinitely, causing the entire launch to hang.
How to Reproduce

Create a case where some ranks get killed but rank 0 stays alive—e.

sglang.title: Wrap IPv6 addresses in gRPC, bench_serving, and log messages
sglang.author: psaab
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Several places in the codebase construct host:port strings using raw f-string concatenation (e.g., f"{host}:{port}"). This breaks when host is an IPv6 address like ::1 or 2001:db8::1, because the colons in the address get confused with the host:port separator. For example, f"http://::1:30

sglang.title: [HiCache] fix: graceful shutdown of pending async tasks in bench_mix.py
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Even when the bench_mix.py benchmark completes successfully with valid results, the end of the run prints a large number of misleading Request failed: Event loop is closed / Task was destroyed but it is pending errors. This is confusing because the benchmark actually succeeded — the error

sglang.title: Clean up _wait_for_scheduler_ready implementation
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Follow-up cleanup for #20287. The previous fix correctly solved the hang-on-dead-rank problem but used sequential per-rank polling — it waits for rank 0 first, then rank 1, etc. If rank 0 is slow but alive while a higher rank dies, detection is delayed until the current rank's poll times

sglang.title: fix cuda graph capturing error in sm120 mxfp8 triton path
sglang.author: wolfcomos
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Previous PR #19112 introduced cuda graph capturing crash error:

PyTorch Dynamo can't trace @lru_cache-wrapped functions. The error occurred because is_sm100_supported() and is_sm120_supported() were called inside compiled code paths.
Modifications
Pre-compute is_sm120_supported() values

sglang.title: [sgl] disable piecewise cuda graph when a model doesn't have layers
sglang.author: bixue2010
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Bug: AttributeError: 'LlamaModel' object has no attribute 'layers' in init_piecewise_cuda_graphs() when eagle3 was enabled alongside enforce_piecewise_cuda_graph=True.
Why: SGLang normally auto-disables piecewise CUDA graph when speculative decoding is active (line 1068 in server_args.py)

sglang.title: [Feature] Optimizations for JPEG input on NVIDIA GPU
sglang.author: wili-65535
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This is the first part of (#18784 and #18559).
Corresponding information and performance data is also there.

Modifications

Use torch.ops.image.decode_jpegs_cuda, converting CPU bytes directly to torch GPU tensors using the nvJPEG hardware decoder.
This eliminates intermediate data form

sglang.title: [Perf] Optimize CUDA IPC for multimodal transfer by caching IPC pool handles
sglang.author: saatwiknagpal
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When CUDA IPC transport is enabled for multimodal models (SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1), every scheduler iteration re-opens IPC handles for the same long-lived memory pool via torch.UntypedStorage._new_shared_cuda(). While the underlying cuIpcOpenMemHandle driver call does reference-co

sglang.title: [Fix] SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1 and SGLANG_ENABLE_MM_SPLITTING=1 do not work at the same time.
sglang.author: wili-65535
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Discuss in the issue19893.
Modifications

Extracts and unpacks the CudaIpcTensorTransportProxy object before creating expanded_mm_items, then restores it afterwards. This ensures proper per-image hashing while preserving the CUDA IPC transport optimization.

Accuracy Tests

sglang.title: Fix circular reference in CustomTestCase.__init_subclass__
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

The safe_setUpClass wrapper captured a bound method via default parameter (_orig=setup), which holds a reference to cls through __self__, creating a reference cycle: cls → setUpClass → safe_setUpClass → _orig → __self__ → cls
This caused dill serialization failures when pickling CustomLogit

Mamba cache not properly sharded for PP: The mamba cache pool allocated memory for all layers regardless of the PP rank. This

sglang.title: [Fix] Handle pre-release tags in nightly wheel version parsing
sglang.author: Kangyan-Zhou
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

The nightly wheel build (build-nightly-wheel job) fails when the latest git tag contains a pre-release suffix like v0.5.10rc0
cut -d. -f3 extracts 10rc0 from the version, and bash arithmetic $((10rc0 + 1)) fails with value too great for base (error token is "10rc0")
This produces an invalid

vllm.title: [Transformers v5] fix missing pixtral/voxtral multimodal dispatch
vllm.author: ghost
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
fix #38382
Transformers decides which processor components to call by looking at the processor constructor and mistral processors only show tokenizer.
This made it so the pixtral image processor and voxtral feature extractor stopped running, but vllm still got text tokens, just no mm kwargs.
